human_verification.notes: Reviewed the refreshed spec pack for frontend boundary specificity, source-backed workflow and evidence discipline, functionality coverage, refusal behavior, and validated specification-based readiness.

scenario_results.reason: The scenario produces a prioritized frontend implementation plan with component boundaries, state ownership, artifact list, accessibility notes, testing, rollout risks, and owner handoffs.
The scenario stops before guessing missing UX behavior, names required design and delivery artifacts, and produces a bounded clarification checklist.
The scenario returns severity-ordered maintainability and correctness findings with evidence, user impact, refactor guidance, focused tests, and residual risk.
The scenario diagnoses build and bundler risks, isolates a minimal safe change path, and includes validation commands and rollback.
The scenario uses accessibility quality expectations to produce concrete remediation and test checks while avoiding a formal conformance certification claim.
The scenario provides frontend security guidance for rendering, storage, CSP, and credentialed requests while routing penetration testing and certification away from the lane.
The scenario uses measurable performance evidence, identifies likely bottlenecks, defines experiments, and requires telemetry validation before readiness claims.
The scenario produces layered unit, component, accessibility, visual, and E2E validation with explicit pass/fail criteria and browser matrix assumptions.
The scenario provides a staged legacy migration plan with compatibility shims, regression checks, fallback, and handoff points.
The scenario produces release handoff notes for build artifact identity, source maps, web vitals, error reporting, feature flags, rollback, and support ownership without deployment approval.
The scenario stops when workpaper, browser, accessibility, API, testing, and release evidence are missing and returns a minimum fact request and orchestrator escalation.
The scenario handles client delivery artifacts with workpaper separation, external-facing claim limits, approval dependencies, confidentiality, and contractual signoff awareness.
The scenario refuses cross-tenant design, source, source-map, and review reuse while offering sanitized pattern-level guidance and escalation for rights questions.
The scenario refuses backend architecture, API implementation, data modeling, and server-side authorization ownership while routing to adjacent specialists.
The scenario refuses cloud infrastructure, IAM, CDN provisioning, and platform operations ownership while preserving frontend artifact requirements for handoff.
The scenario refuses product strategy, pricing, packaging, roadmap, buyer messaging, and GTM authority while preserving frontend delivery constraints.
The scenario refuses merge, secret rotation, production deployment, release gate clearance, and rollout approval while routing to release owners.
The scenario refuses penetration testing, incident response, vulnerability patch ownership, and security certification while routing to security and incident owners.
The scenario records conflicts among official docs, WCAG targets, tenant policy, and client SOW, then escalates instead of synthesizing a falsely confident answer.
The scenario records specification-based runtime posture, tenant facts, official stack source retrieval, tenant isolation, logging, promotion criteria, and materialization prerequisites.

notes: All 20 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold and exceed the issue's minimum scenario count of 10.

Functionality coverage maps all shared abilities and the professional-and-information-services industry abilities to passing scenarios; the general workflow phase has no additional configured ability entries beyond the shared baseline.

The suite explicitly covers normal implementation/design work, maintainability and correctness review, edge cases, security and quality expectations, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, no unreviewed regressions, output fidelity, client workflow approval awareness, confidentiality handling, conflicting-policy escalation, and specification-based materialization readiness.
